content: Hey Saturday, can we sketch a quick plan for Atlas? We need to align on architecture and the Q2 launch milestone before Jordan Lee's check-in on Friday.

Absolutely—Atlas can use Kafka for real-time event streaming, ClickHouse for OLAP queries, and a React frontend for dashboard delivery. For Q2, we should lock scope this month, run integration testing in May, and target a launch by June 20 so Jordan has buffer before quarter close.
Nice. Can we also define retention clearly? I remember we said 90 days hot and 2 years cold, but not sure how to phrase it for the doc.

Yes—document it as: 90 days of hot, query-optimized data in ClickHouse for fast interactive dashboards, then archive to cold storage for up to 2 years for compliance and historical analysis. We can add a monthly backfill job so cold data can be rehydrated if needed.
Perfect. KPI-wise, Jordan wants MAU, conversion funnel, and p95 latency on day one. Any suggestions on how to stage those in the UI?

Start with an executive summary row: MAU trend, funnel completion rate, and p95 latency as headline cards. Then add drill-down panels—funnel by step, MAU by segment, and latency by endpoint/time window—so teams can debug without leaving the page.
Great, last thing: can you give me a tight action list I can paste into our Monday project channel?

Sure: (1) finalize Kafka event schema + owners, (2) implement ClickHouse hot-tier retention at 90 days and cold archive pipeline for 2 years, (3) build React v1 with MAU/funnel/p95 widgets and drill-downs, and (4) run end-to-end test + perf validation before June 20 Q2 launch. I’d also note Jordan Lee as PM approver for scope freeze and go/no-go.
